tasks.title: Eliminate new API methods for opting in to compiler-generated types - We can simplify this to re-using existing methods in the API.
Match all compiler generated types that derive from a list of bases
ActivatorUtilities.CreateFactory created breaking change from version 4.2.2 to 5.0.1 #235
Allow registering fully closed generic types, even if arity doesn't match #104
Filter out IEnumerables when registering as implemented interfaces. #65
Add a fallback to LINQ for search evaluators/validator in case of custom user specifications. (#514)
Consolidate OneOrMany implementation and its usage. (#513)
Refactor the state of order expressions as OneOrMany. (#509)
Use Func to pass custom exception to Guard methods (#350)
WIP: Converting Exception params to exceptionCreator funcs.
Make type parameter T of the Null() method nullable and add overload for value types. (#261)
Fixing changes related to merge conflicts
Add NullOrEmpty and NullOrWhiteSpace overloads for ReadOnlySpan<char> (#237)
Add ShouldBe/ShouldNotBe overloads for Span<T> and ReadOnlySpan<T> (#1221)
Add ShouldSatisfy and make ShouldSatisfyAllConditions an obsolete alias (#1219)
Use Uri.Equals for ShouldBeEquivalentTo Uri comparisons (#1205) (#1215)
Make public api and tests the same for all TFMs (#885)
Fixed issue with writing where an IEnumerable was getting projected twice.
Remove constraint on T for ParseArguments with factory (#590)
feat(PropertyChain): add GetParentChain() to resolve parent property path
Fix `ScheduledTaskExecutor` deadlock when `TrySetResult` runs continuations inline (#2953)
Allow treating unsuccessful status code as non-error, avoid setting exception (#2311)
Fix dashboard JobDataMap and SimpleSchedule trigger display (#3131)
Speed up cron engine with a bitmask CronField (#3127)
Add public plugin configuration extensibility helpers (main) (#3105)
Allow the dashboard Blazor circuit anonymous access under a fail-closed FallbackPolicy + docs (#3117) (#3121)
Allow configuring the Quartz health check (name, tags, failure status) (#3115)
